Dhurandhar[5] is a 2025 Indian Hindi-language spy and action thriller film written, co-produced, and directed by Aditya Dhar. It is produced by Jyoti Deshpande, Aditya Dhar, and Lokesh Dhar under Jio Studios and B62 Studios. The film features an ensemble cast consisting of Ranveer Singh, Akshaye Khanna, Sanjay Dutt, Arjun Rampal, R. Madhavan, Sara Arjun, Rakesh Bedi, Gaurav Gera, and Danish Pandor alongside several supporting actors.[6][7] The first instalment of a duology, it centres on a high-stakes covert counter-terrorism operation.[7] The film follows an undercover Indian intelligence agent who infiltrates Karachiâs criminal syndicates and political power structures in Pakistan in an effort to dismantle a terror network targeting India. The filmâs storyline draws loose inspiration from multiple real-life geopolitical events and conflicts in South Asia, including the 1999 IC-814 hijacking, the 2001 Indian Parliament attack, the 2008 Mumbai attacks, and developments linked to Pakistanâs Operation Lyari.[8][9] Principal photography for Dhurandhar took place from July 2024 to October 2025 across multiple locations in India and abroad, including the states and union territories of Punjab, Maharashtra, Chandigarh, Ladakh and Himachal Pradesh, as well as Thailand. The film features music composed by Shashwat Sachdev, cinematography by Vikash Nowlakha, and editing by Shivkumar V. Panicker. With a runtime of 214 minutes, Dhurandhar ranks as one of the longest Indian films ever produced.[10]

Dimitar Savatinov is a Bulgarian strongman competitor and former professional circus performer celebrated for his old-school approach to strength and his impressive competitive resume. Born in Bulgaria, he developed an early fascination with strength feats and physical performance before relocating to the United States to pursue training and competition at a higher level. His background combines traditional strength ideals with modern strongman techniques, allowing him to excel in demanding events such as the log press, deadlift variations, yoke carries, and loading disciplines that require both brute power and conditioning.

Before gaining recognition in international strongman competitions, Savatinov performed as a strongman with the Ringling Bros. and Barnum & Bailey Circus, where he introduced massive audiences to classic feats of strength and showmanship. That experience helped shape his distinctive presence in the sport, blending theatrical confidence with legitimate athletic ability. Known for his professionalism, work ethic, and respect for strongman history, Savatinov is often admired for keeping the spirit of the traditional strongman alive while continuing to compete and represent the sport at an elite level.
